title: dalbergia obtusifolia
intro: Dalbergia obtusifolia, also known as the Philippine rosewood or Molave, is a tropical tree species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dalbergia obtusifolia include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia obtusifolia typically involve decoctions or infusions of the bark or roots, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
